title: Group Therapy
seo_title: Group Therapy for People-Pleasers in Leesburg, VA

blocks.title: You look like you have it all together. But inside? You're drowning.
blocks.body:
You show up for everyone. You say yes when you want to scream no. You carry everyone else's emotions while yours pile up in the corner, ignored and heavy.

And the worst part? Nobody even knows you're struggling because you're too busy making sure THEY'RE okay.

> If you're tired of being the person everyone leans on while you quietly fall apart, you're in the right place.

blocks.title: This Group Is For You If:

items.title: You're doing too much
items.text: for everyone except yourself. You're not resentful yet, but you're close. You feel the weight building.

items.title: You struggle to say no
items.text: without feeling guilty. Every boundary feels like you're letting someone down.

items.title: You're exhausted
items.text: from constantly monitoring everyone else's needs while yours go unmet.

items.title: You feel invisible
items.text: even when you're surrounded by people. Because they see what you DO for them, not who you ARE.

items.title: Deep down, you're actually terrified
items.text: of being "too much" or "a burden," so you stay small and helpful instead.

blocks.title: What You'll Learn In This Group
blocks.subtitle: "Holding It Together"
blocks.body:
This isn't therapy where you just talk about your feelings and nothing changes.

You'll learn practical skills you can use the moment anxiety or perfectionism kicks into overdrive. Things like:

Grounding techniques that bring you back to YOUR body when you're spiraling in someone else's drama. You'll practice feeling your feet on the floor, your breath in your chest, being present in YOUR moment instead of everyone else's chaos.

The pause practice. Before you automatically say yes, before you jump into fix-it mode, you'll learn to ask: "What's here for ME right now? What within me needs my attention?" And actually respond to that instead of abandoning yourself.

Tolerating being seen. Not just SEEING everyone else, but letting yourself be witnessed. Learning that your needs aren't annoying. Your struggles aren't complaining. That weight you're carrying? It IS heavy. And you're allowed to say so.

## How This Group Actually Works

### The Details

* 6-week closed therapy group
* In-person in Leesburg, Virginia
* Every Tuesday at 6:00 PM (75-minute sessions)
* Starting May 6th, 2026
* Small group: 5 to 8 participants maximum
* Investment: $495 for the complete 6-week program

### What Each Session Looks Like

This is a trauma-informed, connection-based group. That means we're not forcing vulnerability or pushing you to share before you're ready.

Each session includes:

* Intentional guided discussions where everyone participates at their comfort level
* Practical grounding and mindfulness techniques you can use immediately
* Emotional and embodiment practices that help you stay present with yourself
* Space to practice being authentic without performing or people-pleasing
* Real connection with others who actually understand what you're going through

## Why Group Therapy Works Better Than Going It Alone

You'll finally stop feeling like you're the only one struggling with this. Hearing others share similar experiences breaks the isolation that keeps you stuck.

You'll learn from watching others set boundaries and practice saying no. Sometimes seeing someone else do it first makes it feel possible for YOU.

Sharing your story in a safe space IS healing. Not performative sharing, but real "this is what's actually happening" honesty.

You'll build compassion for yourself by extending it to others first. It's easier to see that someone else isn't "too much" before you can believe it about yourself.

blocks.title: Common Concerns (And Real Answers)
blocks.body:
> "What if I don't know what to say?"

You don't have to have it all figured out. There's zero pressure to share anything you're not ready for. Sometimes just listening and being present is enough.

> "What if I don't want to talk about my past?"

You don't have to. This group focuses on what's happening NOW and building skills for moving forward. Your past is yours to share or not share.

> "What if everyone else has bigger problems than me?"

This thinking right here? This is the people-pleaser talking. Your struggles matter. Your exhaustion is real. You don't need permission to take up space here.

> Can I really commit to 6 weeks?"

That's the question you need to answer honestly. This group requires commitment because transformation happens through consistency, not one-off sessions. If you can't commit to every Tuesday at 6 PM for 6 weeks (barring emergencies), this might not be the right timing for you.

blocks.title: Is group right for you?
blocks.body:
##### *"What if I don't know what to say?"*

You don't have to have it all figured out. There's zero pressure to share anything you're not ready for. Sometimes just listening and being present is enough.

##### *"What if I don't want to talk about my past?"*

You don't have to. This group focuses on what's happening NOW and building skills for moving forward. Your past is yours to share or not share.

##### *"What if everyone else has bigger problems than me?"*

This thinking right here? This is the people-pleaser talking. Your struggles matter. Your exhaustion is real. You don't need permission to take up space here.

##### "Can I really commit to 6 weeks?"

That's the question you need to answer honestly. This group requires commitment because transformation happens through consistency, not one-off sessions. If you can't commit to every Tuesday at 6 PM for 6 weeks (barring emergencies), this might not be the right timing for you.

* Are dealing with active substance abuse issues
* Are currently in a domestic violence situation
* Are in crisis mode from recent severe trauma
* Struggle with extreme emotional dysregulation that hasn't been addressed
* Tend to dominate conversations and can't tolerate being redirected
* Have significant untreated personality disorder symptoms
* Can't commit to the full 6-week program

*If you're unsure whether this is right for you, that's what the screening call is for.*
blocks.title: This group is NOT for you if you:

items.title: Step 3
items.text: Once approved, secure your spot with payment

items.title: Step 4
items.text: Show up on Tuesday, May 6th at 6 PM ready to finally put yourself first

subtitle: Connection & healing in community.
